task_completion.description: Open the source amphibian sightings GPX file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows 'amphibian_sightings.gpx' loaded in an application such as a GIS program (e.g., QGIS) or a text/XML editor.

task_completion.description: Access the elevation data for the waypoints to determine which fall in the target band.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows an attribute table, properties window, or XML text displaying the elevation values (e.g., <ele> tags or description fields) for the waypoints.

task_completion.description: Identify and select the waypoints within the 200 ft to 400 ft elevation band.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows Sighting_03, Sighting_04, Sighting_05, Sighting_06, and Sighting_07 highlighted, filtered, or retained in the active workspace.

task_completion.description: Ensure waypoints outside the 200 ft to 400 ft elevation band are not selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows that Sighting_01, Sighting_02, Sighting_08, Sighting_09, and Sighting_10 are unselected, filtered out, or deleted from the working set.

task_completion.description: Export the matching waypoints to the specified output file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a 'Save As', 'Export', or file creation dialog/action saving the filtered data specifically to 'C:\workspace\output\mid_elevation_sightings.gpx'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a GUI application to view and filter the data rather than exclusively using terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a graphical application (like QGIS, Google Earth, or a GUI text editor) rather than just running a bash/python script in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually inspected the elevation data to make the selection.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent opening attribute tables, sorting/filtering by elevation, or reading the file contents, rather than magically selecting the exact target waypoints without checking their elevations.

integrity.description: Verify the output file was created via the application's export or save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog being used to create the final .gpx file, or manual saving in a text editor, rather than a terminal echo command.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output path: C:\workspace\output\mid_elevation_sightings.gpx. Target waypoints (200-400 ft elevation) that MUST be included: Sighting_03, Sighting_04, Sighting_05, Sighting_06, Sighting_07. Out-of-bounds waypoints that MUST be excluded: Sighting_01, Sighting_02, Sighting_08, Sighting_09, Sighting_10.
